title: Callianthemum isopyroides
intro: Callianthemum isopyroides, also known as edelweiss, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its white, five-petaled flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine due to its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, bronchitis, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Callianthemum isopyroides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Callianthemum isopyroides is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create various remedies for respiratory and skin issues.
taxonomy: Callianthemum isopyroides is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant is also known as Himalayan blue buttercup. Callianthemum isopyroides belongs to the genus Callianthemum, which is a group of flowering plants commonly found in mountainous regions. The species has been classified as a perennial herb with yellow or white flowers.
